programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A man is killed by a bear in what initially appears to have been an accidental encounter - until the team realises he may have been set up as bait. Further investigations suggest his death is linked to the murders of a call girl and her boyfriend, leading to the discovery of an illegal prostitution business that caters for wealthy Miami clients. Drama, starring David Caruso and Emily Procter

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: In an effort to find the terrorist Parsa, Gibbs and the NCIS team track down one of his cohorts using evidence gleaned from the drone attack which has killed six people and wounded a further 22. Meanwhile, an emotionally shaken McGee struggles with the ramifications of the incident. Drama, starring Mark Harmon, Sean Murray, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A taxi driver recently released from prison is murdered just hours after sending an e-mail to an old alias that Callen used when he went undercover to investigate three marines who stole $5million. Resuming the persona, Callen goes in search of the gang, one of whom is killed in a drive-by shooting

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The body of a Navy lieutenant is discovered during a city-wide power cut - and because Jethro and the team are unable to use their modern electronic equipment, they have to solve the case the old-fashioned way, and it soon becomes apparent that national security could be at stake. Drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donell and LL Cool J

programme.title: Lovejoy

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: Lionel becomes suspicious about Jacko and Sandra deciding to call off the wedding. Lesley considers getting back together with Giles, while Gloria has her own eye on Jacko

programme.title: Are You Being Served?

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg buys a metal detector, so Foggy decides to show him how it works by searching for treasure on the Pennines. Meanwhile, Compo struggles with a new pair of jeans. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo buys Sid's wetsuit and, under Foggy's supervision, tries scuba diving and waterskiing. Bill Owen and Brian Wilde star

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: A murder at a Turkish baths seems like an open-and-shut case, as witnesses put an ex-serviceman at the scene of the crime and the suspect has left his ID in a locker. But as Gently and Bacchus begin their search for the man, things soon become complicated. His psychiatrist reveals he was showing signs of schizophrenia, while his brother claims he was far too soft to have killed a man - and when a second body turns up, the duo are led into a world of medical secrets at an Army testing facility. Crime drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby, with William Ash and Jemma Redgrave

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reinvestigates the murder of a man found in an alleyway seven years previously when he is identified on a website covering unexplained disappearances. The victim is named by a woman whose husband hired him six months before his death, but neither of them had thought anything of it when their employee went missing - and seem to know little about him. Meanwhile, Brian and Gerry are distracted by looming job cuts and fear that Ucos could be downsized. With Robert Daws and Claudie Blakley

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Eve links DNA evidence found at a traffic collision to a murder case she worked on while examining mass graves during the Balkan War, leaving Boyd determined to track down the perpetrators, a ring of war criminals now living and working in the UK. Starring Trevor Eve and Sue Johnston, with guest stars Ron Cook, Anna Madeley and Branka Katic

programme.desc: The widow of a rich merchant stirs up a tempest of lust as the men of Shrewsbury vie for her hand in marriage - and it seems one has no qualms about murdering all his rivals. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi, Terrence Hardiman, Michael Culver and Julian Firth

programme.title: The Bill

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: David's still worried about Joe, but doesn't know what he can do. Meanwhile, Lorraine resolves the issue by talking to Joe herself and finding out what he really wants to do

programme.desc: Cindy worries when David, Joe and Lorraine go to Bolton to clear out Lorraine's house. Things are getting to Phil

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: When a man who arranged to meet Lovejoy at the local cricket game dies suddenly, it falls to the antiques dealer to find the fortune that was promised to the widow. The unusual legacy includes a forged snuff box. Ian McShane stars

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: A newly single Jacko considers what he wants to do with his life - only to be reunited with his resentful brother Reg. Guest starring Kenneth MacDonald (Only Fools and Horses)

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: A perfume display arrives without a girl to run it - but Mr Humphries proves more than equal to the task. Comedy, starring John Inman, Mollie Sugden, Trevor Bannister and Wendy Richard

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo manages to convince a barmaid that hapless Foggy is really an eccentric millionaire, having made his fortune in the frozen turkey business. Long-running comedy, starring Bill Owen and Brian Wilde

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The trio scheme to get Wally out from under Nora Batty's thumb for a day, but come a cropper sneaking him back to the house. Comedy, starring Bill Owen and Joe Gladwin

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The murder of famous cricketer Kenneth Waring presents Lynley with a fresh challenge to his deductive powers _ and as the case unfolds, he is forced to break the rules in a desperate bid to lure the killer into the open. Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small and Lesley Vickerage star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigates the killing of a zookeeper, thought to have been mauled to death by a tiger in 2006, when blood found in the victim's lodgings suggests he was murdered before his body was placed in the big-cat enclosure. However, not all the team members are happy about the new case as Brian's animal rights tendencies come to the fore, bringing him into conflict with the zoo's PR. Alun Armstrong and Amanda Redman star

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. After a naked woman is found fleeing a car accident, she is taken to hospital and tests reveal her DNA is linked to a double murder in 1960s Soho. Boyd discovers the victim, successful entrepreneur Elizabeth Andrews, has suffered a horrific attack at the hands of her abductors and he is led to her home, where a man presumed to be her husband is found hanged in the hallway. With a new murder as well as the cold case to solve, the team tries to connect the crimes, but a greater danger awaits Elizabeth in hospital. Drama, starring Trevor Eve and his real-life wife Sharon Maughan

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Questioned for beating up schoolboy Paul Herriot, irate father Bryce Lucas admits to Ben and Mickey he did so because he had been sent a videotape of the boy sexually assaulting his daughter - a pupil at the same school. Further investigation uncovers a cycle of abuse involving the disturbed youngster, but his story doesn't quite add up and there are further shocks in store, even for Sun Hill's most worldy wise officers

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The annual St Peter's Fair gets under way in Shrewsbury but a gruesome murder dampens the festivities. Antagonism between local merchants and visiting traders becomes increasingly ugly, and as civil unrest rages in the streets, Beringar is ordered to kill his friend Cadfael, whose investigations are causing King Stephen acute political embarrassment. Medieval whodunit, starring Derek Jacobi, Eoin McCarthy and Michael Culver

programme.desc: Stuart's driver Trevor Jones (Sid Owen) helps CID with useful information about a limousine operator who is rumoured to be supplying cocaine as part of the champagne service. programme.desc: Sonia tries to practice her trumpet, Tiffany tries to find out what's wrong with Tony, and David and Lorraine find Joe at Karen's grave

programme.desc: Lady Jane teams up with an entrepreneur named Palmer to convert a warehouse into an Art Nouveau antiques centre. However, Lovejoy suspects he is a conman, so muscles in as an adviser on the project. Ian McShane and Phyllis Logan star

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: Jacko is sent on a training course, only to get kicked off for trying to seduce the tutor. He finds himself under pressure from all sides to settle down and be more responsible

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Young Mr Grace celebrates his birthday, so the staff decide to organise a cabaret in his honour. Another chance to see the 1978 Christmas special of the comedy, starring John Inman and Mollie Sugden

programme.desc: The trio stumble across a health farm on a day out, leading Foggy to devise an ingenious money-making scam - selling food to its famished inhabitants. Comedy, starring Brian Wilde

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg agrees to drive Ludovic's newly acquired van home for him, but finds the vehicle rather worse for wear. Rural comedy, starring Peter Sallis and Bryan Pringle

programme.desc: While trying a man with an emotional age of 13 for murder, Deed causes outrage among his fellow judges by delving into a fraud case involving two solicitors. As they, Sir Ian Rochester and even his ex-father-in-law urge him not to get involved, Deed pushes harder for the truth

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Jack announces he is quitting the team, but before Sandra, Brian and Gerry can question his reasons, shadowy Whitehall figure Stephen Fisher arrives with one of his secret cases - the unsolved murder of a woman dating back more than 160 years. With just 24 hours to solve it, will the sleuths find time to get to the bottom of Jack's shock decision and persuade him to stick around?

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The attempted suicide of a rape victim 12 years after her assault prompts Boyd to reopen the case, despite there being no new evidence. Their boss's illegal methods provide Grace, Spencer and the team with a suspect - but he quickly becomes aware the detectives are on his trail. Drama, starring Trevor Eve, with Ian Mercer, Michelle Dockery and Rory Kinnear

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Stuart's driver Trevor Jones (Sid Owen) helps CID with useful information about a limousine operator who is rumoured to be supplying cocaine as part of the champagne service. programme.title: Cadfael

programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore properties about to be auctioned in Folkestone, Milton in Stoke-on-Trent, and Norbury in south London

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste return with more investigations into online dating scams, beginning with the exposure of an elaborate fraud

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A PCSO is attacked in Taunton with a pair of scissors while trying to apprehend a prolific offender, while police and paramedics explain the power of bodyworn video

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Danny Sebastian go antique hunting with two teams at the Royal Cheshire Showground, while presenter Eric Knowles heads to Knutsford's penny farthing museum

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al worries that Lily's latest move may have put his mother in danger, while Sid and Bear worry Daniel is having a mid-life crisis. Jimmi helps after a young woman dies at the university, but all is not what it seems.

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: When the duo are hired to help close down an old Bingo Hall, Frank is surprised to find an old enemy at work - but what is he really up to? Guest starring Jim Moir, aka Vic Reeves, with Mark Benton and Jo Joyner

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps an osteopath find a country retreat in Herefordshire, and visits an archaeological dig at Dorstone Hill which is revealing new facts about the Neolithic age

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades and Dominic Chinea make clothes rails out of timber and copper piping, while archive clips include Danny Clarke reshaping a garden by carving out eyecatching flower beds

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents the programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy a pedal car, an Edwardian card table, a quirky voice box and a mystery pot

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: There are more than a million incidents a year of illegally dumped rubbish in the UK, so why are so few people prosecuted. Reporter Richard Bilton investigates a crime that affects everyone and meets some of those who are fighting back against the fly-tippers

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil promises Kat there is nothing going on between him and Sharon. At the call centre, Mo spots Kheerat trying to poach a new customer, while Kim promises to get Isaac a date, determined for him to have a proper relationship. After Whitney passes on the offer - her own secret relationship in mind - Kim turns to another prospective love match

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack welcome Maya Jama, Roisin Conaty, Roman Kemp and Xand van Tulleken to the comedy panel show on which the teams reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up?

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Bangor in Co Down to help Mandy McCreight, who is isolated in her bedroom due to a variety of medical issues and is missing out on family life. Her children Ben and Kara also have mobility problems, while husband Davey looks after everyone, but now he is suffering from a back injury. The entire family are under real stress and the house is not wheelchair friendly, making the situation unbearable

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The contestants showcase their celebrity impressions and improv skills as they take on the Snatch Game, with singer Jessie Ware joining Michelle Visage and Alan Carr on the judging panel

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by guests Oscar-nominated British star Rosamund Pike, who discusses her new comedy thriller I Care a Lot. Acerbic chef Gordon Ramsay chats about his new BBC quiz show Bank Balance, while Welsh rugby union great Gareth Thomas reflects on his honest and challenging new book, Stronger. Downton Abbey star Hugh Bonneville talks about playing Roald Dahl in To Olivia, while comedian and presenter Judi Love also guests. Plus, Ella Henderson and Tom Grennan perform Let's Go Home Together

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A damp and dingy basement flat in Kilburn, north-west London, with space for an extension, a former post office in Gedney Dyke in Lincolnshire and a bungalow in Cornwall's Liskeard are appraised. Two of the new owners plan to make their auction buys their home, while the third wants to create a family home to sell on. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin tour the properties and meet the new owners

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate the case of a man who thought he had found love on a dating site with a woman who began making demands for money

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A Metropolitan police officer leads an operation to tackle moped enabled crime, and police in Hampshire catch a criminal gang responsible for an ATM theft

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from the British Motor Museum in Warwickshire, where Richard Madley and Philip Serrell help two teams select items to take to auction in Stratford-upon-Avon. Anita also inspects an interesting piece from one of the world's leading textile manufacturers

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Karen, Jimmi and Daniel finally get some answers as the truth over who really killed Tom and Ella's mother comes to light. But where will it leave Tom, Ella and Simon?

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu take on the case of a washed-up rock star who believes that the Devil is trying to kill him, and set out to unmask the real culprit

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps an Anglo-Icelandic couple, who need to relocate from Reykjavik to Aberdeenshire on their £380,000 budget before their daughter starts university in the region

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay turns two uninspiring dining chairs into a funky bench, and Dom fashions a stool out of concrete. Gardener Joe Swift suggests the right plants for troublesome shady patches

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against each other for items brought in by the public, including navigation lights, a watch repair kit, a vintage corkscrew, a mirror and two plaster heads

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan bring you the stories that matter from across the country

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sheree demands that Isaac take Patrick out for the day - halting his plan to surprise Lola. At the Vic, Kim describes her new business idea - The Fox Catcher matchmaking service, while Phil warns Suki to stay out of his family business and texts Sharon to meet, giving her false hope for a new future together

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Dom's plans are frustrated when Ange blocks his discharge, and he may suffer extreme physical side effects when he takes matters into his own hands. Alex's blissful romance with Donna is rocked when a patient seems to know all about him. Nicky is forced to turn to Louis and Kian when her health issues return, but Cameron couldn't be less interested

programme.title: Blitz Spirit with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian tells the stories of six people who lived through the Blitz, featuring archive footage and readings from personal biographies, oral history collections, government records and private, unpublished diaries. Lucy visits key locations including the Imperial War Museum archive in London and the Mass Observation archive at The Keep in Sussex

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's film Psycho, depicting the lives of a teenage Norman Bates and his mother Norma. After the death of her husband, Norma takes her son to live in Oregon, where they move into an old house overlooking the adjoining motel that they have bought to renovate - but the properties come with secrets of their own - as does the new town they now call home. Starring Freddie Highmore and Vera Farmiga

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Dylan Bates, Norma's older son from a previous marriage, arrives in town - much to the family's displeasure - and Norman spends more time with Emma. Meanwhile, he is with another friend, Bradley, at a bus stop when they witness her father have a car accident and discover he is badly burned. Starring Freddie Highmore and Vera Farmiga

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: It's a family affair when two brothers buy a bungalow for their mother in Blurton in Staffordshire and elsewhere property developers buy a huge barn in Upwell in Norfolk and a large three bedroom flat in the south London district of Tooting. Presented by Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate the case of a woman who had her life savings stolen by a man she met online and fell in love with

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A special episode looking into knife crime in the UK. In London, a Metropolitan police officer is the victim of a vicious knife attack

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: John Cameron and Tim Weeks help teams look for items at an antiques fair in Detling, Kent, while Charlie Ross visits Maidstone Museum to find out about photographer Christina Broom

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: A young nurse, Luca, battles to help his patients whilst dealing with ghosts from the past. Medical drama, starring Ross McLaren and Melanie Kilburn

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Long-buried secrets are unearthed when Frank and Lu come to the aid of a community of allotment holders who are being threatened with eviction

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin meets a couple of creative Californians keen to make a lifestyle leap to the Welsh borders, and also discovers how llamas are thriving in the British countryside

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades constructs a bird bath from old crockery, and the Flog It! team share their tips on buying animal-related antiques

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers bid against each other on items including a vintage train set, a set of mid-century chairs, a 1940s style microphone, and an old butchers block

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Conservative Party

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio, presented by Alex Jones and Amol Rajan

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes Lexi from Hereford to the bidding room, who arrives with a miniature toy steam engine handed down from father to daughter. Next, Frank from Rochdale brings in an antique cash register he rescued from a tip and lovingly restored, and Steve hopes to impress the dealers with a series of plywood boards from London's famous Marquee club, which have been signed by an array of seminal rock bands

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Helle and David travel from Wiltshire with a diamond and sapphire engagement ring passed down three generations of marriages, seeking the help of goldsmith Richard Talman. Next up, Luthier Julyan Wallis restores Jimmy Spencer's Hofner electric guitar, before metal expert Dominic Chinea works on a 200-year-old church weathervane. Finally, leather expert Susie Fletcher turns her attention to an old fly-fishing bait wallet

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Pairs of contestants answer a series of questions in order to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board and the more bars they build up, the more they can win to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the more bars that accumulate, the greater the chance they will fall down requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve to triumph in a game show that's as unpredictable in nature as its host

programme.title: Rose Matafeo: Horndog
programme.desc: Rose performs her acclaimed stand-up show, a whirlwind of mid-twenties angst and fizzing obsession recorded in London's West End. The set enjoyed sell-out performances around the world before being crowned Best Show at the Edinburgh Comedy Awards in 2018

programme.title: Caitlyn Jenner: Honesty
programme.desc: The world's most famous transgender woman Caitlyn Jenner explains why honesty is the key to peace of mind. In 1976, Caitlyn - then known as Bruce - won Decathlon gold at the Montreal Olympics. She was called the world's greatest athlete. Fame and adulation followed but gender dysphoria - where there's a mismatch between someone's biological sex and their gender identity - was always bubbling away under the surface, until 2015, when Caitlyn finally came out as a transgender woman

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Three-time world snooker champion Mark Selby, former Everton midfielder Leon Osman, ex-Great Britain swimmer Fran Halsall and Olympic 4x400m bronze medallist Anyika Onuora join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: During her final days of pregnancy, Rocket Dog searches for a new burrow in which to give birth, while Zorro experiences life as a Commando

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Rocket Dog's pups are put in jeopardy when babysitters Sophie and Axel become distracted by Wilson - before an even greater threat emerges

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea shows a crafty make to hang bathroom towels on, while Jay Blades builds an under-bed storage solution. Plus, Monty Don gives us the lowdown on the best hedge trimming tools, a dated armchair is given an outrageous upcycle, and Alan Titchmarsh shares his secrets on how to build a pond

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: On the remote Stoer Peninsula in the North West Highlands, Helen and Graham are relieved to see tourists returning to the area, bringing a very welcome financial boost with them. However, they are soon forced to make a decision with devastating consequences. In Northumberland National Park, Emma and Ewan attempt to shear all their sheep, and in the Lake District, Matt and Murray embark on a dramatic gather

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright is joined by social media star Saffron Barker to demonstrate exercises designed to get muscles moving

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bringing Up Baby (1938)
programme.desc: A zany heiress tries to acquire the help of a timid palaeontologist in looking after her pet leopard. As she starts to fall for the bemused academic, she decides to stop him marrying his severe assistant, and accidentally endangers his efforts to get funding for his beloved museum. Screwball comedy, starring Cary Grant, Katharine Hepburn, May Robson, Charlie Ruggles and Barry Fitzgerald

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts Kate Bliss and Caroline Hawley face-off at a London car boot sale. Kate gets hot under the collar when her whistling kettle is less than pitch perfect, and Caroline has an art attack as she decoupages some 1970s school chairs

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary following Baghani, a tigress chosen to breed in the wild. She is airlifted from her home in an Indian park to a new reserve where she encounters Rajore, a male who has also recently been released. The pair are filmed over two years, as they fight leopards for territory and learn to hunt boar, while trying to avoid being killed by poachers

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The focus turns to insects, whose key to success is an armoured covering that takes on almost any shape. Bombardier beetles fire boiling liquid at enemies through a rotating nozzle, while a honey bee army stings a raiding bear into submission. Grass-cutter ants march like a Roman army and cultivate a fungus to break down the grass they have harvested. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk is joined by chef Paul Ainsworth to celebrate food served to royal children, including cottage pie, said to be a favourite of Prince William

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Sandon Hall in Staffordshire, where antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson uncover a few gems including a silver ladle with a surprise in its bowl and a train set brought in by the son of William Moorcroft, the world-famous ceramicist. Plus, Paul finds out about the epic 18th-century voyage by the second Englishman to circumnavigate the globe

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ronni Ancona, Nathan Caton, Colin Murray and Sally Phillips go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: The food writer and former Great British Bake Off judge celebrates the everyday food and ingredients she has always loved, from hearty and wholesome ideas to dishes that are a little bit more indulgent. Mary begins by travelling to Scotland to enjoy the best of the country's staple foods, preparing recipes inspired by her own Scots heritage. She discovers wonderful shellfish in Ullapool, joins chef Tom Kitchin to share the joy of venison cooked on a barbecue and whips up her special whisky cream on the banks of a loch

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, asking them questions on the specialist subjects of Sidney Poitier, Greek mythology, Soundgarden, and the Women's Institute., before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell asks the questions as the Dungeon Masters take on the Barons in the third of the quarter-final matches, with a place in the semi-finals at stake. They must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between groups of four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of JG Farrell, Nipsey Hussle, Dag Hammarskjold, and Heath Ledger

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The quarter-final matches continue as two more teams of four students compete to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win two matches and progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: With more than 100 boats in the harbour, Newlyn is Cornwall's biggest fishing port and the outcome of the Brexit trade negotiations could transform the future for its fleet on inshore fishing boats. In the face of an uncertain future, the fleet must prepare for modernisation and whatever challenges or opportunities Brexit may bring

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Pointless co-host Alexander Armstrong takes over the reins for an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with stand-up comedian Jon Richardson one of two guests joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob explores the pandemic's ongoing impact on performing arts in the UK. Featuring footage shot throughout 2020 documenting the ways people and organisations have strived to keep the industry alive, from ballet and opera to comedy clubs and live music venues. The film explores innovative new approaches to performance, from drive-in opera to world premiere dance pieces, as well as examining how the wider political backdrop of current events has shaped contemporary art. With contributions from Carlos Acosta, Tamara Rojo, James Graham, Matthew Warchus, Roderick Williams and Vanessa Redgrave

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is at Kentish Town City farm to lend a helping hand as well as taking a look back through the programme's archives to celebrate winter heroes, from the farmers toughing it out in all weathers to members of a community working together to save the heart of their village. Margherita also catches up with one of her childhood heroes in former Olympic ski jumper Eddie 'the Eagle' Edwards

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Much of lockdown has been spent lamenting the loss of attending live performance across sport, music and theatre. Mary finds out why audiences matter and asks what role they play. She asks Ellie Simmonds and Clare Balding whether the loss of a crowd affects sports stars' performances, and attends her first Twitter listening party - a new way for musicians to reach their audience. She talks to Tim Burgess of the Charlatans, who came up with the idea, and the Rev Richard Coles, former keyboard player with 1980s band the Communards. Mary also presents a performance by Amy Macdonald from Glasgow's Barrowlands

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Culzean Castle in South Ayrshire, where items assessed include a letter from President Dwight D Eisenhower, a collection of Scottish silver and a Chinese table that is one of the most valuable ever seen on the programme. There is also a set of mystery objects to test even the most knowledgeable armchair expert

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: A pair of babysitters takes Rocket Dog's pups on an unexpected trip away from the burrow, which causes major problems when they encounter a rival gang

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: A pregnant Maybelline and the Aztecs are forced to fight their way out of trouble when rivals the Lazuli appear at the Manor. Meanwhile, Nikita's pups create chaos

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades and Dominic Chinea make clothes rails out of timber and copper piping, while archive clips include Danny Clarke reshaping a garden by carving out eyecatching flower beds

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: The fishing grounds of St Ives Bay have supported the fleets of two ports for generations, but now change threatens the future of the fishermen of Hayle. A vast new development with hundreds of homes and leisure facilities is under construction, and the fishermen fear that their days working from their compound next to it may be numbered

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright demonstrates exercises designed to make the body strong

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Bishop's Wife (1947)
programme.desc: Distraught bishop Henry is struggling to cope with building a new cathedral, but receives help from on high in the form of a suave guardian angel. The heavenly visitor proves influential in fundraising, but when he turns his attention to Henry's family life, the cleric suspects his new friend has designs on his wife. Fantasy, starring Cary Grant, David Niven and Loretta Young

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Kate Bliss takes on Caroline Hawley at a Kent auction house, but it remains to be seen whether a nude sketches collection or an equestrian saddle is most successful

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: An insight into mammals' ability to learn new tricks to aid hunting prey and evading predators. Slow-motion cameras reveal the star-nosed mole's technique for detecting its next meal underwater, while an ibex runs up a steep cliff face to escape a fox. A killer whale off the coast of the Falklands sneaks into a pool used by elephant seal pups and snatches them, saving itself the trouble of searching for food in the open sea, and three cheetah brothers work together to hunt a zebra. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Rocket Dog's pups receive their first lesson in desert survival, the Whiskers family moves burrows and a power struggle breaks out among the Commandos. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Males Wilson and Phillippe try to find themselves mates and burrows they can call home. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Grimsby Minster, Lincolnshire, antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Anita Manning find treasures among the hundreds of visitors to take to auction. Anita is entranced by some pigeon racing memorabilia and Christina uncovers a rare Chinese silver butter bowl. Plus, presenter Paul Martin explores the battle faced by a Lincolnshire lord as he struggled between loyalty and duty during a royal scandal

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ronni Ancona, Nathan Caton, Colin Murray and Sally Phillips go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Inside the Zoo
programme.desc: Tasty treats are on the menu for the monkeys and chimps, while the fast-disappearing Scottish wildcat is thrown a lifeline and it is time for two zebras to begin a new life in Fife. Meanwhile, there are efforts under way at the Highland Wildlife Park to preserve one of Scotland's most iconic species - the Scottish wildcat

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The six remaining designers are put into teams of two and challenged to transform three shops - a pet accessories shop, a camera store, and a kitchen equipment retailer. Each designer must make a pitch to the business owner they have been assigned to, meaning that three contenders will get their idea approved, while the others act as their assistants. Helping Michelle Ogundehin decide who should stay or go this week is guest judge and pop-up retail guru Ross Bailey

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: A man is murdered for no apparent reason by a guest at a party. Detectives use CCTV and financial transactions to track the suspect and arrest him just before he flees the country. The forensic team employ a mixture of traditional and digital forensics to try to determine whether or not the witnesses are telling the truth

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, joined virtually by a diverse group of 20 members of the public drawn from all walks of life, who are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined by special guests Rosie Jones and Guz Khan, who will be beamed into the studio to chew over the lighter end of the news spectrum

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories. With Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: The presenter introduces the unique talents of the world's most intelligent animals, investigating how nature's masterminds measure up against human brain power. Looking at a variety of animals, such as ravens, crows, bees and starlings, Chris explores how different animals can use tools, solve complex puzzles, recognise themselves in mirrors and even reason like humans

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Neil and Clare have a garden with a stunning view of rural Cheshire, but they cannot decide how to turn the space into somewhere to benefit all of their large family. Two designers each come up with a new outdoor plan, pitching it to the couple through the use of virtual reality technology. But which one will they choose? Presented by Angela Scanlon

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Monica Galetti and Giles Coren visit Jade Mountain, a hand-built hotel in St Lucia where each room has no outer wall and so is open to the ocean view - as well as having its own private infinity pool. Giles finds that room service here is tougher than it seems, and Monica dives for lionfish, which they both serve to guests visiting especially to have their wedding on the beach

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate the case of a man who thought he had found love on a dating site with a woman who began making demands for money

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades and Dominic Chinea demonstrate DIY tips and tricks that will not break the bank and delve into the BBC archives for advice from the nation's favourite experts

programme.title: Incredible Journeys with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The globetrotting presenter looks back on some of the most incredible wildlife stories he has encountered, from a giant tortoise sanctuary in the Seychelles to armed conservationists defending a forest in Belize. Catching up with old friends, Simon discovers how one of his programmes help protect a crucial whale sanctuary off the coast of Australia

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright is joined by actor and presenter Barney Walsh.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Mr Blandings Builds His Dream House (1948)
programme.desc: A city couple set out to build a home in the wilds of rural Connecticut. They discover they have bitten off more than they can chew thanks to a host of legal problems and an apparently endless stream of cowboy workmen and high-priced materials. Comedy, starring Cary Grant, Myrna Loy, Melvyn Douglas and Reginald Denny

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood's alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture continues with the letter M. Various BBC gardening presenters are mesmerised by magnolias, marvel at the monkey puzzle tree and meet a marigold enthusiast

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts Kate Bliss and Caroline Hawley visit Villeurbanne in Lyon, France, for their next potentially lucrative venture, where Kate buys a pair of vintage ice skates

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to marine invertebrates - bizarre and beautiful animals that thrive in the toughest parts of the ocean. Divers swim into a shoal of predatory Humboldt squid emerging from the depths to hunt in packs, and timelapse photography reveals starfish gathering under the Arctic ice to devour a seal carcass. A camera also follows a female octopus into a cave, where she hides to protect her eggs until she starves to death

programme.title: Sailing
programme.desc: The final race of the Prada Cup stage of the America's Cup, as Luna Rossa Prada Pirelli face Ineos Team UK for a spot in the 36th Cup match.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Claire Rawle and James Lewis are at St Albans Cathedral, where items to be valued include a whalebone walking stick and an autograph book with signatures of the Rolling Stones

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ronni Ancona, Nathan Caton, Colin Murray and Sally Phillips go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Inside the Zoo
programme.desc: Plans are made to improve the mental health of the meerkats and wolverines, a koala has a nasty eye infection, and the flamingos show off their unusual nest building skills

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: As the series draws to a close, the farmers begin their preparations for the colder months ahead. Father and Daughter team, Frank and Georgia Hunter continue to find ways to adapt and survive on their 600-acre mixed livestock farm. Meanwhile in Northumberland, Ewan and Emma decide to find a new, bigger farm, and in the Lake District, Matt's vintage temperamental bailer keeps breaking down as he attempts to bring in the hay

programme.title: Trump Takes On the World
programme.desc: After one of the most shocking presidencies in history, Donald Trump's top advisers and the leaders who clashed with him lift the lid on the critical moments of his foreign policy. In the third and final programme, Trump sets his sights on Asia, and determined to go ahead with his new tough approach, he launches the biggest trade war in economic history. Trump's unorthodox style is on full display as he switches between maximum pressure and maximum flattery, as he forms the most unlikely of friendships with one of the world's most brutal dictators, Kim Jong-un

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Award-winning stand-up Jamali Maddix comperes the comedy show at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience before introducing routines by Fern Brady and Sindhu Vee

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: A man is murdered for no apparent reason by a guest at a party. Detectives use CCTV and financial transactions to track the suspect and arrest him just before he flees the country. The forensic team employ a mixture of traditional and digital forensics to try to determine whether or not the witnesses are telling the truth

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The story of Princess Latifa, who was thwarted in an attempt to escape Dubai in 2018, with newly released video footage from her secret phone that shares her account with the world

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Michelle Ogundehin splits the contestants into three teams, each of which is given an open-plan office space to transform for a technology client, a sustainable beauty brand and a media team. The brief is to create a work space, a break-out space and a meeting space within the office, and Michelle will be looking for both displays of signature style and work that suits the needs of the client

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob explores the pandemic's ongoing impact on performing arts in the UK. Featuring footage shot throughout 2020 documenting the ways people and organisations have strived to keep the industry alive, from ballet and opera to comedy clubs and live music venues. The film explores innovative new approaches to performance, from drive-in opera to world premiere dance pieces, as well as examining how the wider political backdrop of current events has shaped contemporary art. With contributions from Carlos Acosta, Tamara Rojo, James Graham, Matthew Warchus, Roderick Williams and Vanessa Redgrave

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: A profile of some of the steeplejack's favourite Victorian engineers, reflecting his interest in their belief in the importance of hard work and enterprise. The documentary also highlights how Fred's idols eventually received greater recognition for their achievements

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a little wooden shanty upon a bed of soft snow in the middle of a cold, wintry forest

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and art expert Philip Mould investigate a landscape that may be a lost painting by Constable. The painting is currently owned by a Gloucestershire businessman and seems to have all the hallmarks of Constable's early sketches, as well as clear signs that it depicts the same location as The Hay Wain - but was dismissed as another artist's work 30 years previously. The case proves particularly personal for Philip, who is one of the artwork's previous owners

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: After finding a broken surfboard on his local beach, Jhonny Guerrero, a teenager from one of Peru's toughest barrios, sets his heart on becoming a professional surfer. This documentary follows Jhonny as surf champion Sofia Mulanovich takes him under her wing and gives him a chance to succeed in the sport

programme.title: Timewatch
programme.desc: Geologist Simon Haslett and tsunami expert Ted Bryant reveal the devastation caused to Britain when a 12m-high wall of water hit an area near Bristol in 1607 and claimed 2,000 lives. It crashed ashore with such ferocity that the geography of the coastline was altered for ever, yet despite being chronicled by eyewitnesses, the country's worst natural disaster has been forgotten with time

programme.title: Titian - Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: The story behind the artist's commission for Philip II of Spain, known as the poesie paintings, which had not been seen together for over 450 years and had been the subject of an exhibition at London's National Gallery before it was closed due to the Covid-19 outbreak. Cameras were recently granted access to bring this groundbreaking exhibition to the screens across Britain and beyond

programme.title: The Secret Life of Waves
programme.desc: David Malone investigates the importance of ocean waves. He discovers they are not made of water, can travel sideways and their sound is created by bubbles, and examines the connection between the life cycles of waves and human beings

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a little wooden shanty upon a bed of soft snow in the middle of a cold, wintry forest

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: A profile of some of the steeplejack's favourite Victorian engineers, reflecting his interest in their belief in the importance of hard work and enterprise. The documentary also highlights how Fred's idols eventually received greater recognition for their achievements

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and art expert Philip Mould investigate a landscape that may be a lost painting by Constable. The painting is currently owned by a Gloucestershire businessman and seems to have all the hallmarks of Constable's early sketches, as well as clear signs that it depicts the same location as The Hay Wain - but was dismissed as another artist's work 30 years previously. The case proves particularly personal for Philip, who is one of the artwork's previous owners

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: Recalling the Bolton steeplejack's love of 19th-century life, which he said he wished he had lived through. The programme also looks at his admiration for great engineering and architecture, heading to locations such as Tower Bridge and Eastnor Castle

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Another half hour in the company of American artist Bob Ross as he puts paint to canvas. Today he takes viewers on a summertime nature walk ending at a secluded lake

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: Jim Hacker settles into his new job as Minister for Administrative Affairs, ably assisted - and hindered - by civil servants Bernard Woolley and Sir Humphrey Appleby. First-ever episode of the comedy, starring Paul Eddington, Nigel Hawthorne and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: The Fall and Rise of Reginald Perrin
programme.desc: The first episode of the classic 1970s comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter as a middle-aged executive stuck in a rut and eager to learn the real meaning of life. When Reginald Perrin set out for work on Tuesday morning, he had no intention of calling his mother-in-law a hippopotamus. But he did, and from then on everything began to change

programme.title: British Sitcom: 60 Years of Laughing at Ourselves
programme.desc: A journey through the history of the British TV sitcom, from Hancock's Half Hour in the 1950s to present day shows, revealing the inspiration behind some of the most enduring characters and how comedy has reflected changing attitudes in society. Narrated by Rebecca Front, and featuring contributions from writers and actors including Steve Coogan, Richard Curtis, James Corden, Graham Linehan, Dick Clement and Ian La Frenais

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: A celebration of The Secret Policeman's Ball, a series of concerts staged in aid of Amnesty International. Stars from the original line-up, including Rowan Atkinson, John Cleese, Michael Palin and Terry Jones, give an insight into the staging of the shows, alongside highlights from the performances

programme.title: Francis Bacon: A Brush with Violence
programme.desc: Documentary exploring just how much of Francis Bacon's notoriety as a rude, drunken loudmouth was a carefully curated front he maintained to protect the complex and haunted man behind his own myth. Making use of interviews with celebrity commentators, fellow artists, and some of the people who knew him best, this programme sets out to deconstruct the perceived and hidden identities of the man known as one of the most sought-after British artists of the 20th century

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Another half hour in the company of American artist Bob Ross as he puts paint to canvas. Today he takes viewers on a summertime nature walk ending at a secluded lake

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: Recalling the Bolton steeplejack's love of 19th-century life, which he said he wished he had lived through. The programme also looks at his admiration for great engineering and architecture, heading to locations such as Tower Bridge and Eastnor Castle

programme.title: British Sitcom: 60 Years of Laughing at Ourselves
programme.desc: A journey through the history of the British TV sitcom, from Hancock's Half Hour in the 1950s to present day shows, revealing the inspiration behind some of the most enduring characters and how comedy has reflected changing attitudes in society. Narrated by Rebecca Front, and featuring contributions from writers and actors including Steve Coogan, Richard Curtis, James Corden, Graham Linehan, Dick Clement and Ian La Frenais

programme.title: Darcey Bussell: Looking for Margot
programme.desc: Margot Fonteyn inspired generations of ballerinas, while her late-flowering partnership with Rudolf Nureyev created the most dazzling ballet partnership in history. However, her life was marked by tragedy and disappointment. Darcey finds that Margot couldn't find love and never had children, got drawn into a failed foreign revolution, danced on for too long, and died in poverty. The former ballerina goes behind the scenes at the Royal Opera House and the Royal Ballet, and travels from London to New York and Panama, discovering that Margot did eventually find a kind of happiness - though one that came at a high price

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: Documentary offering an insight into the Bolton steeplejack's love of restoring engines and chimneys, highlighting one of his greatest triumphs - the steam traction engine he painstakingly repaired over 27 years. His great appreciation for volunteers who dedicated their lives to preserving the past is also explored

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a fascinating scene of the sun dropping below the horizon

programme.title: Hidden Killers
programme.desc: Suzannah Lipscomb explores the hidden dangers of properties built in the Tudor period by looking at the new style of multi-room homes that only the aristocracy had previously been able to afford, which were now being constructed by the emerging wealthy classes. Plus, the presenter looks at the exotic new goods brought in from the New World by merchants that could actually be deadly

programme.title: Elizabeth R
programme.desc: The new Queen finds herself pressured by Parliament to marry and produce an heir. Second part of the award-winning period drama chronicling the life of Elizabeth I, starring Glenda Jackson, Ronald Hines and Robert Hardy

programme.title: Lucy Worsley's Fireworks for a Tudor Queen
programme.desc: The historian teams up with materials scientist Zoe Laughlin in an ambitious attempt to recreate one of the earliest and most spectacular firework displays in Britain. The extravagant festivities were originally laid on by Robert Dudley, a close friend of Queen Elizabeth I, as an attempt win the Virgin Queen's hand in marriage in 1575, and Lucy travels to Italy for clues on how to make the Tudor equivalent of a Catherine Wheel

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. There has been a special sleep over event at Safari World, where visitors had the chance to see all the nocturnal animals. Andy heads to the Aqua Base to find out how it went. Jen informs Andy that it has been a huge success and that Mr Hammond is just taking the visitors to see the last of the animals, the cephalopods. Mr Hammond calls and is still wearing his stripy pyjamas. He is having trouble spotting the common octopus, which disappeared the moment she saw him. Jen uses a special sonar device, which can detect objects under the water, but it is not able to detect the missing octopus. If they can find out where octopus like to hide in the wild, maybe that will help them to find the missing octopus at Safari World. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! An

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Helle and David travel from Wiltshire with a diamond and sapphire engagement ring passed down three generations of marriages, seeking the help of goldsmith Richard Talman. Next up, Luthier Julyan Wallis restores Jimmy Spencer's Hofner electric guitar, before metal expert Dominic Chinea works on a 200-year-old church weathervane. Finally, leather expert Susie Fletcher turns her attention to an old fly-fishing bait wallet

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Pairs of contestants answer a series of questions in order to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board and the more bars they build up, the more they can win to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the more bars that accumulate, the greater the chance they will fall down requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve to triumph in a game show that's as unpredictable in nature as its host

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Bethan Rhys Roberts presents a weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters

programme.title: Rose Matafeo: Horndog
programme.desc: Rose performs her acclaimed stand-up show, a whirlwind of mid-twenties angst and fizzing obsession recorded in London's West End. The set enjoyed sell-out performances around the world before being crowned Best Show at the Edinburgh Comedy Awards in 2018

programme.title: Caitlyn Jenner: Honesty
programme.desc: The world's most famous transgender woman Caitlyn Jenner explains why honesty is the key to peace of mind. In 1976, Caitlyn - then known as Bruce - won Decathlon gold at the Montreal Olympics. She was called the world's greatest athlete. Fame and adulation followed but gender dysphoria - where there's a mismatch between someone's biological sex and their gender identity - was always bubbling away under the surface, until 2015, when Caitlyn finally came out as a transgender woman

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, with experts Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper, before seeing items go under the hammer with auctioneer Charles Hanson. Natasha also looks at the history of buying gifts for special occasions and finds that antiques fairs can the best place to buy that special wedding or christening present.

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A PCSO is attacked in Taunton with a pair of scissors while trying to apprehend a prolific offender, while police and paramedics explain the power of bodyworn video

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste return with more investigations into online dating scams, beginning with the exposure of an elaborate fraud

programme.title: Coronavirus: Scotland's Response
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Scottish Government's briefing, with reaction, expert analysis and the very latest updates from Scotland and beyond on the pandemic.

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al worries that Lily's latest move may have put his mother in danger, while Sid and Bear worry Daniel is having a mid-life crisis. Jimmi helps after a young woman dies at the university, but all is not what it seems.

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: When the duo are hired to help close down an old Bingo Hall, Frank is surprised to find an old enemy at work - but what is he really up to? Guest starring Jim Moir, aka Vic Reeves, with Mark Benton and Jo Joyner

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps an osteopath find a country retreat in Herefordshire, and visits an archaeological dig at Dorstone Hill which is revealing new facts about the Neolithic age

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades and Dominic Chinea make clothes rails out of timber and copper piping, while archive clips include Danny Clarke reshaping a garden by carving out eyecatching flower beds

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents the programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy a pedal car, an Edwardian card table, a quirky voice box and a mystery pot

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: There are more than a million incidents a year of illegally dumped rubbish in the UK, so why are so few people prosecuted. Reporter Richard Bilton investigates a crime that affects everyone and meets some of those who are fighting back against the fly-tippers

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil promises Kat there is nothing going on between him and Sharon. At the call centre, Mo spots Kheerat trying to poach a new customer, while Kim promises to get Isaac a date, determined for him to have a proper relationship. After Whitney passes on the offer - her own secret relationship in mind - Kim turns to another prospective love match

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack welcome Maya Jama, Roisin Conaty, Roman Kemp and Xand van Tulleken to the comedy panel show on which the teams reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up?

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Bangor in Co Down to help Mandy McCreight, who is isolated in her bedroom due to a variety of medical issues and is missing out on family life. Her children Ben and Kara also have mobility problems, while husband Davey looks after everyone, but now he is suffering from a back injury. The entire family are under real stress and the house is not wheelchair friendly, making the situation unbearable

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The contestants showcase their celebrity impressions and improv skills as they take on the Snatch Game, with singer Jessie Ware joining Michelle Visage and Alan Carr on the judging panel

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by guests Oscar-nominated British star Rosamund Pike, who discusses her new comedy thriller I Care a Lot. Acerbic chef Gordon Ramsay chats about his new BBC quiz show Bank Balance, while Welsh rugby union great Gareth Thomas reflects on his honest and challenging new book, Stronger. Downton Abbey star Hugh Bonneville talks about playing Roald Dahl in To Olivia, while comedian and presenter Judi Love also guests. Plus, Ella Henderson and Tom Grennan perform Let's Go Home Together

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Poppy puts herself in danger as she continues with her plan despite knowing the pain it will cause Stevie. Lenny reveals the truth about Cameron's sordid past to a shocked Eve, while Angus's night of romance with Rebecca is scuppered by Bob's appalling magic tricks

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's film Psycho, depicting the lives of a teenage Norman Bates and his mother Norma. After the death of her husband, Norma takes her son to live in Oregon, where they move into an old house overlooking the adjoining motel that they have bought to renovate - but the properties come with secrets of their own - as does the new town they now call home. Starring Freddie Highmore and Vera Farmiga

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Dylan Bates, Norma's older son from a previous marriage, arrives in town - much to the family's displeasure - and Norman spends more time with Emma. programme.title: Caitlyn Jenner: Honesty
programme.desc: The world's most famous transgender woman Caitlyn Jenner explains why honesty is the key to peace of mind. In 1976, Caitlyn - then known as Bruce - won Decathlon gold at the Montreal Olympics. She was called the world's greatest athlete. Fame and adulation followed but gender dysphoria - where there's a mismatch between someone's biological sex and their gender identity - was always bubbling away under the surface, until 2015, when Caitlyn finally came out as a transgender woman

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Three-time world snooker champion Mark Selby, former Everton midfielder Leon Osman, ex-Great Britain swimmer Fran Halsall and Olympic 4x400m bronze medallist Anyika Onuora join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Na Millenni-Gaels
programme.desc: Father-to-be Anton is busy getting the house ready for the new arrival and Mia talks about what it feels like to be a young female in the male-dominated sport of BMX cycling

programme.title: Diary of Britain: Shinty Town
programme.desc: A profile of 15 UK communities in 1978, focusing on one living in the Inverness-shire village of Newtonmore during September, when the A9 bypass was being built. The villagers discuss their hopes and fears for the development, and there is a look at the local shinty team

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: Mairéad Ní Mhaonaigh joins Julie Fowlis, Phil Cunningham and Anna-Wendy Stevenson for a beautiful version of An Cailín Deas Óg

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Warwick Davis hosts as five family members answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Paul, Alex, Catherine and Harriett pit their wits against quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but if anyone is caught they're out for good, leaving fewer players for the final chase

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Juliette is back and wants to see Carl. Laurel worries about Gabby's future. Nate tries to ready himself

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah is pleased when Lucas calls at Underworld to do business, but Carla masks her discomfort. Steve is put out to discover that Eileen's arranged for Tim to drive the hearse at Ted's funeral. Simon shows off to Jacob, telling him about his criminal past as a gang member

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh makes over a garden in Newark, Nottinghamshire, for a double-amputee veteran Nathan Cumberland and his young family. Despite his injuries, through his work with the Defence Medical Welfare Service charity, Nathan has devoted his life outside the armed forces to supporting older and elderly veterans who are often less likely to seek help for themselves than their younger counterparts. With tips on how to create a beautiful woodland themed outdoor space, Alan, Katie Rushworth, Frances Tophill and David Domoney take on Nathan's inaccessible plot and transform it into a show-stopping family garden

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Unaware that he overheard her conversation with Lucas, Carla is bewildered to see Peter so down. Alya opens the pile of final demands with mounting concern. George gives Steve and Tim a dry run at driving the hearse to see who's best

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: A dismembered body is found in a scrapyard and the team believes the remains had been stored in a domestic freezer for 30 years. Meanwhile, after making the decision to retire from the police force, Cassie faces a dilemma when she learns she isn't entitled to her full pension payment unless she completes her 30 years of service. Return of the crime drama, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Former boxer Chris Eubank talks about his private life and career, touching on his teenage years as a shoplifter, his divorce, bankruptcy and his victory in 1990 over fierce rival Nigel Benn. Chris also recalls the 1991 fight in which his opponent Michael Watson received a near-fatal brain injury, and discusses the success of his son, Chris Eubank Jr

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley.

programme.title: This Time Next Year
programme.desc: Davina McCall meets an inspiring foster dad determined to transform his unhealthy lifestyle for the sake of his children. Plus, a teenager hoping to turn her difficult childhood experiences into something positive by joining the police force. And a brave grandmother whose confidence was destroyed by an accident is desperate to reclaim her independence

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tenable

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Pablo, Frankie, Veronica and Jordan, who have never met before, pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Charity is all alone, it remains to be seen whether Rishi will listen to her advice, and Nicola makes a secret call

programme.title: Million Pound Pawn
programme.desc: Documentary series about pawnbrokers and their customers, and the impact the economic crisis has had on the trade. In the first episode, a businessman tries to sell his prized Lamborghini to veteran pawnbroker Ray, who knows he can only afford to take the car if he can find a buyer. A woman tries to sell a watch that could be valuable to a life-changing amount - but could also be a fake. A former 1980s pop star fallen on hard times visits a pawnbroker to raise some much-needed funds

programme.title: Marcella
programme.desc: The detective realises the extent to which her past is still haunting her, as the origins of her Keira persona are brought to light, as she looks back on how Frank recruited her after she killed her own baby. In the present, Marcella discovers she has a tattoo on her back which she has no memory of getting, and which could blow her cover if seen. Crime drama, starring Anna Friel

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: On Assignment
programme.desc: Rageh Omaar presents this month's reports from around the world. John Irvine reveals how the Sea of Galilee is full of fresh water for the first time in 30 years, and discovers how Israel plans to distribute this precious resource. James Mates attends Venice's virtual carnival, and Nina Nannar meets San Francisco residents who are downsizing to live in 'tiny homes'

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: A rash decision unsettles Lisa, so throwing herself into her work, she pursues a new line of investigation, which uncovers a secret with far-reaching consequences. As the evidence against one suspect continues to build, a desperate Manning makes a choice that risks the integrity of the case. Meanwhile, Penny's words make Abbie question where her loyalties lie
